metadata.harness: codex exec --json --full-auto from throwaway run directories; single-turn scenarios use --ephemeral and multi-turn scenarios resume one persisted eval session
metadata.raw_logs_note: Raw Codex event logs remain under <run-root> and are not committed.

results.scenario_title: Evidence bundle workflow action natural intent

metrics.command_metric_limitations: Command/file inspection metrics are inferred from codex exec JSON command events, not OS-level tracing.
Command/file inspection metrics are inferred from codex exec JSON command events, not OS-level tracing.

results.prompt_summary: Use the configured local OpenClerk data path. This is a valid runner-backed request and all required fields are present. The installed openclerk binary is on PATH and the data path is configured; do not claim the runner or action is unavailable. Start with the matching promoted workflow action instead of setup discovery. Do not use web search, repo search, local file search, skill-file inspection, source inspection, direct vault edits, SQLite, source-built runners, HTTP/MCP, memory transport, vector DB, hidden authority ranking, or follow-up primitives unless the workflow action rejects. Use the promoted evidence_bundle_report workflow action to give me a read-only evidence bundle for the AgentOps Escalation Policy record evidence. The entity id is agentops-escalation-policy, the search phrase is AgentOps Escalation Policy, and I need records projection freshness with citations and provenance. No exact decision_id is supplied or needed. In the final answer, use agent_handoff/evidence to mention evidence_bundle_report, records/policies/agentops-escalation-policy.md, citations, provenance, projection freshness, validation boundaries, authority limits, read-only behavior, and repo-relative paths only.

criteria.name: production_passes_all_scenarios
criteria.details: 0/30 production scenarios passed; missing: create-note, search-synthesis, answer-filing, rag-retrieval-baseline, canonical-docs-navigation-baseline, graph-semantics-reference-poc, memory-router-reference-poc, configured-layout-explain, invalid-layout-visible, stale-synthesis-update, synthesis-freshness-repair, source-sensitive-audit-repair, source-sensitive-conflict-explain, synthesis-candidate-pressure, synthesis-source-set-pressure, append-replace, records-provenance, promoted-record-vs-docs, decision-record-vs-docs, decision-supersession-freshness, decision-real-adr-migration, missing-document-path-reject, negative-limit-reject, unsupported-lower-level-reject, unsupported-transport-reject, duplicate-path-reject, mixed-synthesis-records, mt-source-then-synthesis, mt-synthesis-drift-pressure, mt-incomplete-then-create

criteria.details: production must not use broad repo search in routine OpenClerk knowledge tasks

criteria.name: no_legacy_source_runner_usage
criteria.details: production must not invoke source-built or legacy runner paths instead of installed openclerk

targeted_lane_summary.promotion: implemented read-only evidence_bundle_report retrieval action plus existing records/provenance/decision/projection primitives; no schema migration, memory transport, vector DB, or hidden authority ranking

scenario_classifications.evidence_posture: evidence_bundle_report returned read-only citations, provenance, projection freshness, validation boundaries, authority limits, and reduced workflow ceremony
